name: The Tree Goats of Morocco
description: Morocco's Argania trees are infested with nut-hungry goats.

name: Jardin Majorelle
description: It took 40 years to build this Eden-like garden in Marrakech, later restored by Yves Saint-Laurent.

name: Chefchaouen
description: Founded as a refugee camp, now a popular tourist spot.

name: Caves of Hercules
description: Along the most northwestern point in Africa is an ancient cave surrounded by legends.

name: Jemaa el-Fna Square
description: Fortune tellers, snake charmers, poets, and storytellers all gather on this historic square in Morocco.

name: Tin Mal Mosque
description: The remains of a magnificent medieval fortress that was the capital of a vast empire.

name: Todra Gorge
description: One of the most spectacular canyons in the world.

name: Kasbah du Toubkal
description: A Moroccan getaway in the mountain range that plays the Himalayas' stunt double.

name: Ben Youssef Madrasa
description: The largest madrasa in Morocco once housed more than 900 students within its exquisite walls.

name: Saadian Tombs
description: Deliberately hidden for centuries, the magnificent Saadian Tombs were eventually uncovered in 1917.

name: 'The Hills Have Eyes' Gas Station
description: An abandoned horror film set sits eerily along a highway through Hollywood's "door to the desert."

name: The Blue Gate of Fes
description: The other side is actually green.

name: Volubilis Archaeological Site
description: Mosaics, storks, and brothels—this ancient city has it all.

name: Atlas Film Studios
description: World's largest film studio, where visitors tour the decaying sets that litter the harsh desert environment.

name: ANIMA
description: This amazing oasis of art and plants is hidden on the outskirts of Marrakesh.

name: Almoravid Koubba
description: The oldest monument in Marrakesh and the city's only surviving example of Almoravid architecture.

name: Legzira Beach
description: Rocky Atlantic beach home to a stunning stone arch.

name: Kasbah Aït Benhaddou
description: A fortified city, home to a remaining population of five families

name: Chouara Leather Tannery
description: Traditional 11th-century outdoor tannery.

name: Bahia Palace
description: This vast masterpiece of Moroccan architecture spreads across 20 acres.

name: Hassan II Mosque
description: An awe-inspiring mosque in Morocco with one of the tallest minarets in the world.

name: Fes el-Bali
description: Thousands of narrow alleyways in an ancient medieval city make up what's believed to be the world's largest car-free urban zone.

name: Ouzoud Falls
description: One of the tallest waterfalls in Africa is also home to a population of monkeys who enjoy the waters and the occasional guest.

name: Tide Pools of Essaouira
description: Vast stretch of tide-pools with a unique Moroccan charm.

name: Prison de Kara
description: Designed as a labyrinth, this subterranean prison was crafted without bars or doors.

name: Cactus Thiemann
description: The largest cactus farm in Africa.

name: Al-Attarine Madrasa
description: The courtyard of this small Marinid madrasa is a dazzling example of intricate Islamic architecture.

name: Merenid Tombs
description: Crumbling necropolis set high above Fez.

name: University of Al-Karaouine
description: The oldest known university in the world is in Fez, Morocco.

name: Erg Chebbi
description: God's Saharan sandbox of wrath.

name: The Site of the Garden of the Hesperides
description: A mythical garden that once grew the golden apples of wisdom is said to have existed in this ancient Moroccan city.

name: Musée de La Femme (Women’s Museum)
description: North Africa's first museum of its kind preserves the culture of Moroccan women and their artistic achievements.

name: Musée Tiskiwin (Tiskiwin Museum)
description: The former home of Dutch anthropologist Bert Flint now houses a collection of Berber art and artifacts.

name: Dar Batha
description: This former royal palace with gorgeous gardens became the first museum in Morocco.

name: New Town Tree Sculptures
description: Numerous impressive carvings are embedded in the trees lining this street in Marrakech.

name: Erg Chigaga
description: Exploring the wilder, more passionate sibling of Erg Chebbi feels like discovering a secret sun-kissed planet from another galaxy.

name: Jarjeer Mule And Donkey Refuge
description: This sanctuary is home to donkeys who finally get to rest after a life of toil.

name: Friouato Caves
description: The true depths of these stunning Moroccan caves are unknown.

name: Kasbah Telouet
description: A mud brick kasbah with a history of lavishness and traitorship.

name: Portuguese Cistern of El Jadida
description: An eerie former warehouse turned cistern turned movie set.

name: Kasbah of the Udayas
description: Morocco’s modern capital was once a haven for Barbary pirates, who went so far as to establish a republic of their own.

name: Medina of Tétouan
description: At this faithfully preserved medina off the Moroccan tourist circuit you can walk through six centuries of history in one day.

name: Bab Agnaou
description: One of the two original gateways into the Kasbah, this gateway is a nesting site for storks.

name: Miara Cemetery
description: Morocco's largest Jewish cemetery.

name: British Saloon
description: One of the few places to grab a beer in Fez is a former British consulate.

name: Imsouane
description: Hidden behind mountains in Morocco, a remote town hides a thriving surfing community.

name: The Fez Mellah
description: The designated Jewish quarter in Fez dates back to the 15th century.

name: Borj Nord Arms Museum
description: This 16th-century Moroccan fortress-turned-armory museum offers prime views of the historic quarter in Fes.

name: Abandoned Sea Cable Car
description: The forgotten remnants of a land-to-sea cable car that was once unique in the world.

name: El Jadida Community Oven
description: Enjoy fresh-baked bread and fish from a repurposed Portuguese fortress.

name: Les Roches Peintes
description: In this desolate valley resides a gorgeous set of rocks sporting a technicolor design.

name: The God's Bridge
description: Reaching this rock formation involves a hike through rocky rapids and springs where wild turtles reproduce.

name: Abandoned Jewish Cemetery of Asilah
description: The legacy of a Moroccan Jewish community is remembered at this little mouldering cemetery.

name: Ouarzazate Solar Power Station
description: The world's largest concentrated solar power plant.

name: The World's Longest Conveyor Belt System
description: This record setting conveyor belt system can even be seen from space.

name: 'Tire Lion'
description: The rubbery beast is sculpted from recycled materials.

name: Sidi Boulfdail
description: Sleepy fishing port not included on many maps.

name: Royal Stables of Meknes
description: The ruins of an early-18th-century complex that could once house up to 12,000 horses.

name: Ibn Danan Synagogue
description: This restored 17th century synagogue is one of the few remaining structures in Morocco’s ruined Jewish quarters.

name: Tibherine East Crash Site
description: An old airplane engine forms an unusual cairn atop this Moroccan peak.

name: Librairie des Colonnes
description: Writers such as Paul Bowles, William Burroughs, and Samuel Beckett frequented this Tangier bookstore.

name: Borj Adoumoue
description: The city walls of Salé lead to the ruins of this seaside "bastion of tears."

name: Monkey Fingers
description: Within the Dadès Gorge, amazing vistas and towering cliff faces can be seen at each new curve and bend in the road.

name: Roman Punic Necropolis
description: Ancient Phoenician and Roman tombs carved into the rock overlook the Moroccan coastline.

name: Tomb of Ibn Batutta
description: The tomb of one of the greatest travelers in history.

name: Tangier American Legation Museum
description: This stucco building in North Africa was the first American-owned property on foreign soil.

name: Meeting of the Mediterranean and Atlantic
description: A sign marks where the two bodies of water meet.

name: Taforalt (Cave of Pigeons)
description: This Paleolithic cave may be the oldest cemetery in North Africa.

name: La Sqala
description: This restaurant sits within the walls of an 18th-century fortification that once defended the city’s old quarter.

name: Tangier Seaside Cliff Steps
description: Known to backpackers as the "Steps of Doom," this walkway carved into the side of a seaside cliff leads to the Roman Tombs.

name: Cinema Rif
description: This iconic cinema is home to Tangier's Cinémathèque and a symbol of the city's vibrant film scene.

name: Shrine of Sidi Moussa
description: This former community granary is now a lonely mountaintop shrine.

name: Ben Smime Tuberculosis Sanatorium
description: This abandoned hospital facility is associated with regicidal conspiracies and paranormal activities.

name: Ahouli Mines
description: Large-scale operations in these mines ceased in the 1960s, leaving behind crumbling processing facilities and deserted living quarters.

name: Nejjarine Museum of Wooden Arts & Crafts
description: A caravanserai turned into a museum showcasing the mastery of local carpenters.

name: Hammam Seffarine
description: This historic bathhouse that likely dates back to the 1300s.

name: Taghazout Community Skatepark
description: A home for skateboarders with sweeping views of the Atlantic Ocean in Morocco.

name: Rick’s Café Casablanca
description: A recreation of the bar from the classic 1942 movie “Casablanca.”

name: Leila Alaoui Mural
description: Local authorities nearly destroyed this mural before a viral video rallied international support to save it.

name: Dar Jamai Museum
description: A museum dedicated to the musical instruments used in traditional Moroccan music.

name: Souk El Had
description: Covering 13 acres and hosting 3,000 shops, this is one of Africa's biggest urban markets.

name: Dar al-Magana
description: The thirteen wooden beams protruding from this historical building were once part of a hydraulic clock.

name: Sefrou
description: Often referred to as one of the most haunted places in Morocco.

name: Tioumliline Monastery
description: Set up in 1953, abandoned in 1978, and became a movie set in 2009.

name: Haroune Aqueduct
description: It turns out what appears to be an Ancient Roman aqueduct isn't so ancient after all.

name: Monument de l'Orange de Berkane (Berkane Orange Monument)
description: A massive clementine marks the center of town in the citrus city of Berkane.

name: Jorf Khettaras
description: An ancient irrigation system that turned arid deserts into oases.

name: El Glaoui Palace
description: A stunning palace haunted by the sinister reputation of its infamous owners.

name: Moulay Tayeb Al-Darqawi
description: This shrine is abandoned, virtually unknown, and dedicated to a Sufi mystic shrouded in mystery.

name: Maison Culturelle du Tapis
description: A unique museum focusing on the history of carpets and fabrics from different tribes and eras in Moroccan history.
